page: Microsoft Azure E16-4as_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EASv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The configuration includes 4 virtual CPUs derived from 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is strong, placing in the top 25% with a score of 2701.38. However, multi-core CPU performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% with a score of 5400.07, which is consistent with the lower physical core count of 2. Memory bandwidth benchmarks, including read-only and write operations on large blocks, are positioned in the bottom 25%, though the Passmark memory score remains average. Cryptography and compression tasks, such as Passmark CPU encryption and single-threaded gzip compression, perform in the average tier.

Given the high memory density of 128.0 GB alongside a modest 4 vCPUs, this server is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power. It is suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM but limited CPU scaling. Conversely, it is less optimal for highly parallelized compute tasks, software compilation, or high-throughput web serving, where its multi-core and memory bandwidth limitations may present bottlenecks.
description: Microsoft Azure E16-4as_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EASv5 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores, 2 threads per core) and 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. The instance has no local storage or GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, though multi-core performance and memory bandwidth fall into the lower tier. This resource profile makes the E16-4as_v5 highly cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that do not require extensive parallel processing.

tagline: A memory-dense virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and high RAM allocation for memory-intensive enterprise applications.
